SPAGH_001.name: De-idiomatization / Loop Flattening
SPAGH_001.description: Replace idiomatic constructs (in / range / map.get / sum / ternary) with manual index loops or explicit if-chains.

SPAGH_002.name: Redundant Temporaries
SPAGH_002.description: Introduce a single-use temporary variable for every sub-expression.

SPAGH_003.name: Boolean Verbosity
SPAGH_003.description: Assign true/false explicitly via if/else; introduce redundant '== true' comparisons.

SPAGH_004.name: Dead Code / No-op Injection
SPAGH_004.description: Inject behavior-preserving no-op statements and unreachable branches.

SPAGH_005.name: Cascading Conditionals
SPAGH_005.description: Expand a lookup or selection into a long elif/switch/nested-if chain instead of a single expression.

SPAGH_006.name: Manual Indexing / Pointer Arithmetic
SPAGH_006.description: Use *(ptr+i) / explicit slice indexing instead of iterators or [] semantics.

SPAGH_007.name: Defensive Over-Guarding
SPAGH_007.description: Wrap every access in redundant null/bounds/type checks.

SPAGH_008.name: Verbose Decomposition
SPAGH_008.description: Split a single expression into many statements: explicit accumulators, counters, flags.

SPAGH_009.name: Opaque Predicates
SPAGH_009.description: Guard real code with an always-true predicate that looks data-dependent (n*(n+1) is even). Collberg-Thomborson control-flow obfuscation.

SPAGH_010.name: Redundant Recomputation
SPAGH_010.description: Recompute the loop-invariant collection length on every iteration instead of caching it (loop-invariant de-hoisting): the literal anti-optimization.

SPAGH_011.name: Yoda Conditions
SPAGH_011.description: Flip comparison operand order (constant == variable) in the match / reduce / select check. Classic readability code smell.

_profiles_note: Strictly nested pattern SETS minimal<light<standard<heavy<max (clean = empty baseline). Nesting orders the enabled sets, not necessarily the rendering: consult sites differ per operation, so rendered incidental complexity is monotone but NON-strict. Corpus-wide, 'light' and 'standard' render identically (SPAGH_005 is masked by the always-on SPAGH_001; SPAGH_007 has no consult site in any generator), and lookup-only programs render identically from 'light' upward (their emitters consult only SPAGH_001/002/005). 'light' and 'heavy' were added (v2 Phase C) to give >=6 incidental knob points for judge-monotonicity statistical power; the rendering ties attenuate rather than inflate monotonicity statistics.
